thread.title: West Indies Shine on Day One: Louis and Athanaze Lead with 97 and 90
thread.title_full: West Indies Shine on Day One: Louis and Athanaze Lead with 97 and 90

author: BVM Sportsdesk
title: West Indies Shine on Day One: Louis and Athanaze Lead with 97 and 90
text:
West Indies Shine on Day One: Louis and Athanaze Lead with 97 and 90
West Indies ended day one of the Antigua Test against Bangladesh at 250 for 5, with standout performances from Mikyle Louis, who scored 97, and Alick Athanaze, who contributed 90. The pair's 140-run partnership revitalized the innings after a slow start. Despite losing both batsmen just shy of their centuries, their contributions gave the West Indies a strong foothold in the match. Bangladesh bowlers struggled to contain the West Indies after a disciplined beginning, with Taskin Ahmed taking 2 wickets.By the Numbers
- West Indies scored 250 runs for 5 wickets at the close of play.
- Mikyle Louis faced 218 balls and scored 97 runs; Alick Athanaze scored 90 runs.
Bangladesh kept West Indies' scoring in check for the first 54 overs but struggled significantly during the following sessions. While Taskin and Taijul Islam showed promise, the inability to sustain pressure allowed West Indies to dominate large parts of the day.State of Play
- West Indies had a dominant fourth-wicket partnership that revitalized their innings.
- Bangladesh's bowlers faced challenges, particularly against the added aggression of the West Indies batsmen later in the day.
As play resumes, Bangladesh must regroup and bolster their bowling attack to contain West Indies' remaining batting strength. The precarious position of West Indies at 250 for 5 means that the next few wickets could shift momentum.Bottom Line
Despite a solid day, West Indies' failure to convert near-centuries into milestones highlights the need for sustained performances to ensure a strong lead in the Test. The loss of their two key players creates a crucial juncture for both teams heading into day two.
